title: centaurea aplolepa subsp. pandataria
intro: Centaurea aplolepa subsp. pandataria, also known as a type of thistle, is a plant species within the sunflower family that is native to the Mediterranean region. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome, and its ability to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. Herbal preparations of Centaurea aplolepa subsp. pandataria can be made through the extraction of its essential oils, which can be used in the form of teas, capsules, or topical applications.
